title: OPTIMIZATION OF ULTRASONIC EXTRACTION OF SESQUITERPENE LACTONES FROM THE ROOTS OF INULA GRANDIS
author: Makhkamova Z.
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The factors influencing the process of extraction of sesquiterpene lactones from the roots of  Inula grandis  under the influence of ultrasound were studied using the Box-Wilson mathematical experimental design method.     The following extraction modes were set: ultrasound power: 150 W; ultrasound frequency: 40 kHz; extraction temperature – 50 ºС; ethyl alcohol concentration – 95%; process duration – 20 min; raw material grinding degree – 7 mm. Moreover, the yield of sesquiterpene lactones is more than 70% of the content in the raw material during a single phase contact.

Moreover, the yield of sesquiterpene lactones is more than 70% of the content in the raw material during a single phase contact.
keywords: Inula grandis, sesquiterpene lactones, ultrasonic extraction, the method of mathematical planning of the experiment.
